message.content: Adversaries may exploit the Terminal Services DLL component to maintain persistent access to systems. Terminal Services, also known as Remote Desktop Services, facilitate remote terminal connections via RDP, transmitting a full graphical user interface to clients. The service's DLL file, specified in the ServiceDll registry entry, can be modified or replaced by adversaries to execute arbitrary payloads or enable additional RDP functionalities. This can include enabling concurrent RDP sessions or bypassing user prompts on non-server Windows OSs, thus avoiding detection. Common exploitation methods involve altering the ServiceDll registry value or patching the DLL file to enhance RDP capabilities. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and validating the integrity of the Terminal Services DLL, employing strict access controls on registry keys, and using endpoint detection and response (EDR) tools to detect unusual modifications or behaviors associated with Terminal Services.
